headings: h2: WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON
text_excerpt: PMQs & Parliamentary Tours – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Prime Minister’s Questions (PMQs) PMQs take place every Wednesday at 12:00 PM for 30 minutes. Constituents can apply for tickets through my office. We receive a very limited number of PMQ tickets each month, and they are in high demand, so it is advisable to book some months in advance. Requests are dealt with on a first-come, first-served basis, and will usually be for no more than two tickets per application. To request tickets, please provide the full names and addresses of individuals attending. UK Parliament Tours UK Parliament offers a range of guided tours, giving visitors the chance to explore the building and learn about the work of UK Parliament. Contact my office if you wish to book an Inside UK Parliament tour, which lasts 75 minutes and is free of charge and open to UK residents. The fascinating tour focuses on how Parliament operates, the role of MPs, and key historical and heritage aspects of the Palace of Westminster. Big Ben Tour MPs can book a free official tour of Big Ben on behalf of constituents who want to explore the famous Elizabeth Tower at Parliament. This guided tour takes you up a narrow 334-step spiral stairwell to the belfry where Big Ben, the Great Bell, hangs. You will see the inner workings of the clock mechanism, stand next to the world-famous Big Ben bell as it strikes the hour, and step behind the dials each spanning 6.9m in diameter. Please note, visitors are required to be aged 11 or over by the date of their visit and children aged 11-17 require adult supervision. MPs are only allocated 8 bookings each year. If you’d like to book a tour or PMQs tickets through my office, please contact: jeff.smith.mp@parlaiment.uk Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like Loading...
